structured.research_question:
Does replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing
medium increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to the standard
DMSO protocol?
structured.subject: HeLa cells
structured.independent:
Cryoprotectant identity (trehalose substituted for sucrose in the freezing
medium, vs. standard DMSO-based protocol).
structured.dependent:
Post-thaw cell viability, measured as percentage of viable cells after
thawing.
structured.conditions:
Standard slow-rate freezing in cryovials, storage in liquid nitrogen,
rapid thaw at 37 C, viability measured by trypan blue exclusion 24h
post-thaw. Trehalose at 0.5M, sucrose at 0.5M, DMSO at 10% v/v.
structured.expected:
Trehalose substitution increases post-thaw viability by at least 15
percentage points compared to the DMSO standard, due to trehalose's
superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

procedures.name: HeLa Cell Culture Preparation
procedures.intent: To prepare and expand HeLa cells to a sufficient, healthy, and consistent cell density for cryopreservation experiments.

procedures.name: Cryoprotectant Medium Preparation
procedures.intent: To accurately prepare the experimental cryoprotective media (DMSO, trehalose, sucrose) at the specified concentrations.

procedures.name: Cell Freezing
procedures.intent: To uniformly prepare and freeze HeLa cell aliquots with each cryoprotectant medium using a controlled slow-rate freezing method.

procedures.name: Cell Thawing and Initial Recovery
procedures.intent: To rapidly thaw frozen HeLa cell aliquots and initiate their recovery in culture under standardized conditions.

procedures.name: Post-Thaw Viability Assessment (24h)
procedures.intent: To measure the viability of HeLa cells 24 hours post-thaw using trypan blue exclusion.

overall_assumptions: Standard cell culture equipment (incubators, BSCs, centrifuges, microscopes) is available and functional.
Certified aseptic techniques will be maintained throughout all cell culture procedures.
Appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) will be used.
Reagents and media are of cell culture grade and prepared according to manufacturer instructions unless otherwise specified.
An established, healthy stock of HeLa cells is available for initial culture expansion.
overall_controls: Positive Control: Freshly cultured HeLa cells (not frozen) to establish 100% viability baseline.
Negative Control: HeLa cells frozen without any cryoprotectant (media only), expected to show very low viability.
Standard Control: HeLa cells frozen with 10% DMSO (standard protocol) for comparison against experimental conditions.

procedures.name: HeLa Cell Culture Preparation
procedures.intent: To prepare and expand HeLa cells to a sufficient, healthy, and consistent cell density for cryopreservation experiments.

steps.title: Thaw and Initiate HeLa Cell Culture
steps.body_md: Retrieve a single cryovial of HeLa cells from liquid nitrogen storage. Rapidly thaw the cryovial by immersing it in a 37°C water bath for approximately 2-3 minutes until only a small ice crystal remains. Transfer the thawed cell suspension to a 15 mL conical tube containing 9 mL of pre-warmed complete growth medium (DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS, 1% Penicillin-Streptomycin).
steps.notes: Ensure quick thawing to minimize exposure to DMSO and maximize viability. Gently mix to avoid cell damage.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Resuspend Cells
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature to pellet the cells. Carefully aspirate the supernatant, leaving approximately 0.5 mL of medium. Resuspend the cell pellet gently by pipetting up and down.
steps.notes: Aspirate supernatant carefully to avoid disturbing the cell pellet. This step removes the cryoprotectant (DMSO).

steps.title: Seed Cells into T-75 Flask
steps.body_md: Add 9.5 mL of fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium to the resuspended cell pellet for a total volume of 10 mL. Transfer the entire cell suspension to a T-75 tissue culture flask. Label the flask with the date, cell type, passage number (P1 for this initial culture), and your initials.
steps.notes: Ensure the medium is pre-warmed to prevent temperature shock to the cells.

steps.title: Incubate Cells
steps.body_md: Place the T-75 flask in a humidified tissue culture incubator maintained at 37°C with 5% CO2. Allow the cells to attach and proliferate. Monitor cell growth daily by visual inspection using an inverted microscope.
steps.notes: Cells should be observed for morphology and confluency.

steps.title: Subculture Cells (Passaging)
steps.body_md: When the T-75 flask reaches 70-80% confluency (typically every 2-3 days), aspirate the old medium. Wash the cell monolayer once with 5 mL of sterile 1x PBS. Aspirate PBS completely. Add 2 mL of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA solution to the flask, ensuring the entire monolayer is covered. Incubate at 37°C for 2-5 minutes until cells begin to detach. Gently tap the flask to aid detachment if necessary.
todo_for_researcher: Determine optimal trypsinization time based on specific HeLa cell line characteristics and manufacturer's recommendations.
steps.notes: Do not over-trypsinize cells, as this can lead to cell damage and reduced viability during subsequent steps.

steps.title: Neutralize Trypsin and Collect Cells
steps.body_md: Once cells are detached, add 8 mL of complete growth medium to the flask to neutralize the trypsin. Gently pipette the cell suspension up and down to break up any cell clumps. Transfer the entire cell suspension to a 15 mL conical tube.
steps.notes: Ensure complete neutralization of trypsin to prevent residual proteolytic activity.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Resuspend for Counting
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature. Aspirate the supernatant completely. Resuspend the cell pellet in 1-2 mL of fresh complete growth medium.
steps.notes: The exact resuspension volume should allow for accurate cell counting.

steps.title: Determine Cell Count and Viability
steps.body_md: Take a 10 µL aliquot of the cell suspension and mix it with 10 µL of 0.4% Trypan Blue solution. Count viable and non-viable cells using a hemocytometer or automated cell counter. Calculate the total cell density (cells/mL) and percentage viability. Ensure cells are within passages 5-15.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm the specific passage number range (P5-P15) is appropriate for preventing phenotypic drift in HeLa cells for reproducibility across experiments.
steps.notes: Cell viability should ideally be >90% for subsequent cryopreservation. Discard cells with low viability (<80%).

steps.title: Prepare Cells for Cryopreservation
steps.body_md: Based on the cell count, centrifuge the remaining cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes. Aspirate the supernatant. Resuspend the cell pellet in fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium to achieve the desired cell density of 1 x 10^6 - 2 x 10^6 cells/mL for cryopreservation.
steps.notes: Accurate cell density is crucial for consistent cryopreservation results across experimental groups.

procedures.name: Cryoprotectant Medium Preparation
procedures.intent: To accurately prepare the experimental cryoprotective media (DMSO, trehalose, sucrose) at the specified concentrations.

steps.title: Prepare Trehalose Cryoprotectant Solution (0.5M Trehalose in Basal Medium)
steps.body_md: Weigh out 90.08 g of D-(+)-Trehalose dihydrate. Dissolve the trehalose in approximately 400 mL of the basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS, appropriate for HeLa cells) in a sterile 1 L volumetric flask. Ensure all solids are completely dissolved. Adjust the final volume to 500 mL with basal medium.
reagents_referenced: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS)
steps.notes: The molecular weight of trehalose dihydrate is approximately 360.32 g/mol. For a 0.5M solution in 500 mL, 0.5 mol/L * 0.5 L = 0.25 mol. 0.25 mol * 360.32 g/mol = 90.08 g.

steps.title: Sterilize Trehalose Cryoprotectant Solution
steps.body_md: Filter the 0.5M trehalose solution through a 0.22 µm sterile filter into a sterile bottle. Label the bottle clearly with the solution name, concentration, preparation date, and your initials. Store at 4°C.

steps.title: Prepare Sucrose Cryoprotectant Solution (0.5M Sucrose in Basal Medium)
steps.body_md: Weigh out 85.57 g of D-(+)-Sucrose. Dissolve the sucrose in approximately 400 mL of the basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS) in a sterile 1 L volumetric flask. Ensure all solids are completely dissolved. Adjust the final volume to 500 mL with basal medium.
reagents_referenced: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS)
steps.notes: The molecular weight of sucrose is approximately 342.3 g/mol. For a 0.5M solution in 500 mL, 0.5 mol/L * 0.5 L = 0.25 mol. 0.25 mol * 342.3 g/mol = 85.575 g.

steps.title: Sterilize Sucrose Cryoprotectant Solution
steps.body_md: Filter the 0.5M sucrose solution through a 0.22 µm sterile filter into a sterile bottle. Label the bottle clearly with the solution name, concentration, preparation date, and your initials. Store at 4°C.

steps.title: Prepare DMSO Cryoprotectant Solution (10% v/v DMSO in Basal Medium)
steps.body_md: Aseptically add 50 mL of cell culture grade Dimethyl Sulfoxide (DMSO) to a sterile 500 mL bottle containing approximately 450 mL of basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS). Mix gently by inverting the bottle several times. Do not filter after adding DMSO.
equipment_needed: Sterile serological pipette or equivalent for DMSO dispensing
reagents_referenced: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS)
steps.notes: DMSO should be added to the medium, not the reverse, to ensure proper mixing and reduce heat generation. DMSO is typically sterile-filtered by the manufacturer and is not re-filtered after mixing with media to avoid loss or contamination.

steps.title: Store DMSO Cryoprotectant Solution
steps.body_md: Label the bottle clearly with the solution name, concentration, preparation date, and your initials. Store the 10% DMSO solution at 4°C and protect from light.

equipment: Sterile serological pipette or equivalent for DMSO dispensing
reagents: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS)

deviations_from_source.from_source: The source protocol (260183) specifies preparation of a 'Freezing buffer TDMSO' containing 0.08M Trehalose and 0.5M DMSO for C. elegans.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: This procedure prepares three distinct cryoprotectant media for HeLa cells: 0.5M trehalose, 0.5M sucrose, and 10% v/v DMSO, each in basal cell culture medium.
deviations_from_source.reason: The experimental hypothesis requires specific concentrations of trehalose (0.5M), sucrose (0.5M), and DMSO (10% v/v) as separate cryoprotectants for HeLa cells, rather than a combined solution or the specific concentrations used for C. elegans. The basal medium is also adapted for HeLa cells.

deviations_from_source.from_source: The source protocol mentions 'Buffer M9' as the base for the freezing medium, with specific components for C. elegans.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS) is used as the base for all cryoprotectant solutions.
deviations_from_source.reason: HeLa cells require a standard mammalian cell culture medium. The M9 buffer is specific to C. elegans and unsuitable for HeLa cells, thus a deviation is necessary to match the cell type specified in the hypothesis.

deviations_from_source.from_source: The source protocol describes preparing 500 mL of TDMSO, which includes dissolving Trehalose and DMSO directly into M9 buffer.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: Trehalose and sucrose solutions are prepared separately and filter-sterilized. DMSO is added to pre-sterilized basal medium without further filtration.
deviations_from_source.reason: This adaptation ensures that each cryoprotectant can be tested independently. Trehalose and sucrose are solids requiring dissolution and sterilization, while DMSO is typically purchased as a sterile liquid and should not be filtered after dilution in media due to its properties and potential risks of absorption.

success_criteria.how_measured: Visual inspection of solution clarity and label verification.
success_criteria.threshold: Clear, colorless solution, correctly labeled with 0.5M trehalose concentration.

success_criteria.how_measured: Visual inspection of solution clarity and label verification.
success_criteria.threshold: Clear, colorless solution, correctly labeled with 0.5M sucrose concentration.

success_criteria.how_measured: Visual inspection of solution clarity and label verification.
success_criteria.threshold: Clear, colorless solution, correctly labeled with 10% v/v DMSO concentration.

procedures.name: Cell Freezing
procedures.intent: To uniformly prepare and freeze HeLa cell aliquots with each cryoprotectant medium using a controlled slow-rate freezing method.

steps.title: Prepare HeLa cells for cryopreservation
steps.body_md: Harvest HeLa cells and gently resuspend them in the appropriate growth medium (DMEM with 10% FBS, 1% P/S). The total volume should be sufficient to achieve the desired cell concentration before splitting into cryoprotectant specific groups.
todo_for_researcher: Specify exact harvesting method (e.g., trypsinization duration based on cell confluency and cell type characteristics).
steps.notes: Ensure cells are healthy and in log-phase growth prior to harvesting for optimal cryopreservation outcomes.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells and remove supernatant
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature to pellet the cells. Carefully aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the cell pellet.
steps.notes: Gentle aspiration is crucial to prevent cell loss.

steps.title: Adjust cell concentration
steps.body_md: Resuspend the cell pellet in a minimal volume (e.g., 1-2 mL) of the standard growth medium. Count viable cells using a hemocytometer and trypan blue exclusion. Adjust the cell concentration to 2x10^6 cells/mL in standard growth medium.
steps.notes: The target concentration of 2x10^6 cells/mL ensures 1x10^6 cells/vial when 0.5 mL of cell suspension is added to 0.5 mL of cryoprotectant, resulting in 1x10^6 cells/mL in the final cryopreservation mix.

steps.title: Prepare cryoprotectant media
steps.body_md:
Prepare three cryoprotectant solutions: 
1. DMSO control: 20% (v/v) DMSO in standard growth medium. 
2. Sucrose: 1 M sucrose in standard growth medium. 
3. Trehalose: 1 M trehalose in standard growth medium.
All solutions should be filter-sterilized and chilled to 4°C prior to use.
steps.notes: The final concentration after mixing with cells will be 10% DMSO, 0.5M sucrose, and 0.5M trehalose respectively.

steps.title: Aliquot cells into cryovials with cryoprotectants
steps.body_md: For each experimental group (DMSO, Sucrose, Trehalose), rapidly mix 0.5 mL of the 2x10^6 cells/mL cell suspension with 0.5 mL of the corresponding chilled cryoprotectant medium in separate 1.8 mL cryovials. This results in a final volume of 1 mL per cryovial at a concentration of 1x10^6 cells/mL with the desired cryoprotectant concentration (10% DMSO, 0.5M Sucrose, or 0.5M Trehalose). Once mixed, proceed immediately to the freezing steps, minimizing the time the cells are exposed to the cryoprotectant at room temperature to no more than 15 minutes.
steps.notes: Ensure thorough but gentle mixing to evenly distribute cells without causing damage. Label cryovials clearly with the cryoprotectant type, cell line, date, and passage number.

steps.title: Controlled slow freezing (initial cooling)
steps.body_md: Place the cryovials in a polystyrene box (e.g., Mr. Frosty freezing container or similar) or an equivalent controlled-rate freezing device that achieves a cooling rate of approximately -1°C per minute. Immediately place the box at -80°C for 24 hours.
steps.notes: The polystyrene box insulates the cryovials, ensuring a gradual decrease in temperature instead of flash freezing, which is critical for cell viability. Confirm the specific cooling rate of your device.

steps.title: Transfer to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage
steps.body_md: After 24 hours at -80°C, carefully transfer the cryovials from the -80°C freezer directly into a liquid nitrogen storage tank (vapor phase or liquid phase, depending on standard lab practice). Record the location of each cryovial.
steps.notes: Minimize exposure of cryovials to ambient temperature during transfer to prevent warming, which can impact cell viability. Always wear appropriate PPE (cryogloves, lab coat, safety goggles) when handling liquid nitrogen.

deviations_from_source.from_source: The source protocol is for Cryopreservation of C. elegans.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: This protocol is adapted for HeLa cells.
deviations_from_source.reason: The experimental hypothesis specifies HeLa cells as the subject. Standard mammalian cell culture practices (e.g., cell harvesting, media, and specific cryoprotectant concentrations) are applied.

deviations_from_source.from_source: Source protocol uses M9 buffer and TDMSO with 0.08M Trehalose and 0.5M DMSO (components mentioned in materials text).
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: Adapted to use standard mammalian growth medium (DMEM + FBS + P/S) for preparing cryoprotectant solutions, and specific cryoprotectant concentrations of 10% (v/v) DMSO, 0.5M sucrose, and 0.5M trehalose.
deviations_from_source.reason: HeLa cells require specific growth media. The hypothesis specifies the exact concentrations for DMSO, sucrose, and trehalose to be tested, which are different from the C. elegans protocol's TDMSO buffer.

deviations_from_source.from_source: Source protocol steps 9887402, 9887403, 9887406, 9887407 describe multiple washes with M9 buffer and resuspension in TDMSO.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: The initial cell preparation steps (harvesting, trypsinization, washing) are generalized, focusing on obtaining a healthy, concentrated cell suspension rather than specific buffer washes which are not typical for mammalian cell cryopreservation directly before adding cryoprotectant.
deviations_from_source.reason: Mammalian cell cryopreservation typically involves resuspending cells in growth medium before adding cryoprotectants, rather than multiple washes with a specific buffer like M9.

deviations_from_source.from_source: Source protocol uses 1 ml aliquots into '4 frascos (crioviales)' and implies 1.5 ml cryovials from materials list.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: This adaptation specifies 1.8 mL cryovials and a final volume of 1 mL per vial (0.5 mL cell suspension + 0.5 mL cryoprotectant) to accommodate the targeted cell density of 1x10^6 cells/vial.
deviations_from_source.reason: Standardizing cryovial volume and final suspension volume to ensure consistent cell concentration per vial, as per 'Key params'.

deviations_from_source.from_source: Source protocol step 9887411 mentions placing cryovials at -1°C for 10 to 15 minutes before transferring to -80°C.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: This protocol uses a polystyrene freezing box (e.g., Mr. Frosty) directly at -80°C for 24 hours to achieve a controlled -1°C/min cooling rate, effectively bypassing the intermediate -1°C step.
deviations_from_source.reason: The 'key_params' specify a '1°C/min' freezing rate, which is best achieved for mammalian cells using a controlled rate freezing device or a polystyrene box in a -80°C freezer, rather than a specific -1°C holding step, which is less common for controlled rate freezing of mammalian cells. This method directly translates to a controlled, slow freeze.

deviations_from_source.from_source: Source protocol step 9887413 suggests testing viability in 1 week and then moving some tubes to liquid nitrogen and keeping some at -80°C, and step 9887414 describes thawing directly into an NGM plate or scraping.
deviations_from_source.to_adapted: This protocol focuses on long-term storage in liquid nitrogen after a controlled freeze, and defers post-thaw viability testing and thawing methods to a subsequent procedure, aligning with the scope of 'Cell Freezing'.
deviations_from_source.reason: The 'Cell Freezing' procedure's intent is to prepare and freeze cells, not to thaw or assess viability. Long-term storage in liquid nitrogen is standard for cryopreserved cells, and post-thaw handling is a separate step in the overall experiment.

success_criteria.what: Each cryovial contains 1x10^6 viable cells in 1 mL of final cryopreservation medium.
success_criteria.how_measured: Pre-freezing cell count and viability assessment (Trypan Blue exclusion) before adding cryoprotectant and confirmed calculation of final concentration per vial.
success_criteria.threshold: Greater than 90% viability pre-freeze (typical for healthy cultured cells).

success_criteria.how_measured: Proper use of polystyrene freezing box (e.g., Mr. Frosty) at -80°C or programmatic control of a controlled-rate freezer.
Cryovials are transferred to liquid nitrogen storage after 24 hours at -80°C.
success_criteria.threshold: Storage duration in -80°C not exceeding 36 hours before transfer to LN2; labeled properly.

procedures.name: Cell Thawing and Initial Recovery
procedures.intent: To rapidly thaw frozen HeLa cell aliquots and initiate their recovery in culture under standardized conditions.

steps.title: Prepare Water Bath and Medium
steps.body_md: Pre-warm a water bath to the specified thawing temperature. Also, pre-warm a sufficient volume of complete growth medium in a sterile container to the specified temperature for immediate use after thawing.

steps.title: Retrieve Frozen Vial
steps.body_md: Carefully retrieve one cryovial of frozen HeLa cells from liquid nitrogen storage, handling it quickly to minimize temperature fluctuations. Ensure cryo-gloves and face shield are worn for safety.

steps.title: Rapid Thawing
steps.body_md: Immediately immerse the cryovial in the pre-warmed water bath. Gently agitate the vial until only a small ice crystal remains (approx. 1-2 minutes). Avoid prolonged incubation to prevent cryoprotectant toxicity.

steps.title: Disinfect Vial Exterior
steps.body_md: Remove the vial from the water bath and immediately disinfect its exterior with 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Transfer Cells to Conical Tube
steps.body_md: Under sterile conditions in a laminar flow hood, open the disinfected cryovial and carefully transfer its entire contents (typically 1 mL) to a sterile 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Slow Dilution of Cryoprotectant
steps.body_md: Slowly add 9 mL of pre-warmed complete growth medium dropwise to the conical tube containing the thawed cells, over approximately 1-2 minutes. Gently swirl the tube during addition to ensure even mixing and gradual dilution of the cryoprotectant.

steps.title: Centrifugation
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at the specified speed and duration to pellet the cells. Ensure the centrifuge is balanced.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm optimal centrifuge speed for HeLa cells for gentle pelleting post-thaw

steps.title: Remove Supernatant
steps.body_md: Carefully aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the cell pellet. Retention of a small volume (e.g., 50-100 μL) above the pellet is acceptable.
equipment_needed: Vacuum aspirator with sterile Pasteur pipette

steps.title: Resuspend Cells and Transfer to Culture Vessel
steps.body_md: Gently resuspend the cell pellet in an appropriate volume of fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium (typically 1-2 mL, depending on desired density). Transfer the cell suspension to a sterile tissue culture flask or well plate.
todo_for_researcher: Determine specific culture vessel and exact medium volume based on downstream experimental needs.

steps.title: Incubation
steps.body_md: Place the culture vessel in a humidified CO2 incubator at the specified temperature and CO2 concentration to allow cells to recover.
steps.notes: Cell viability will be assessed 24 hours post-thaw according to the experimental plan.

equipment: Vacuum aspirator with sterile Pasteur pipette

success_criteria.how_measured: Visible cell pellet at the bottom of the conical tube after centrifugation.
Observation under a microscope after 24 hours in culture, cells should show signs of attachment and exhibit typical HeLa cell morphology (e.g., flattened, spreading).
success_criteria.threshold: At least 50% of cells show normal adherence and morphology

success_criteria.how_measured: Microscopic inspection of the culture medium and cells for bacterial, fungal, or yeast contamination after 24 hours.

procedures.name: Post-Thaw Viability Assessment (24h)
procedures.intent: To measure the viability of HeLa cells 24 hours post-thaw using trypan blue exclusion.

steps.title: Prepare cell culture dishes for post-thaw incubation
steps.body_md: Prepare new cell culture dishes or multiwell plates by adding pre-warmed complete growth medium suitable for HeLa cells (e.g., DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS, 1% Penicillin-Streptomycin). Ensure the culture medium is at 37°C before adding cells.
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine appropriate cell culture dish size/format based on experimental design and cell number.
steps.notes: Ensuring the medium is pre-warmed minimizes thermal shock to the thawed cells.

steps.title: Rapid Thawing of Cryovials
steps.body_md: Retrieve cryovials containing frozen HeLa cells from liquid nitrogen storage. Immediately immerse the cryovial in a 37°C water bath. Agitate gently until only a small ice crystal remains (approximately 1-2 minutes). Avoid prolonged incubation in the water bath once thawed.
steps.notes: Rapid thawing is crucial for cell viability. The small ice crystal indicates thawing is complete but avoids overheating the cells.

steps.title: Transfer thawed cells to pre-warmed medium
steps.body_md: Under sterile conditions in a biological safety cabinet, decontaminate the cryovial exterior with 70% ethanol. Unscrew the cap and immediately transfer the entire contents of the cryovial to a sterile tube containing 5-10 mL of pre-warmed complete growth medium. This dilutes the cryoprotectant.
equipment_needed: Sterile conical tube (e.g., 15 mL or 50 mL)
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine exact dilution volume based on cryovial volume and expected cell concentration.
steps.notes: Gradual dilution of cryoprotectants helps prevent osmotic shock and improves cell recovery.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells to remove cryoprotectant
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 200 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature. This will pellet the cells and allow for removal of the supernatant containing the cryoprotectant.
steps.notes: Pelleting cells gently prevents cellular damage. Room temperature centrifugation is sufficient.

steps.title: Resuspend cells and transfer to culture dishes
steps.body_md: Carefully aspirate the supernatant, ensuring not to disturb the cell pellet. Gently resuspend the cell pellet in a small volume (e.g., 1-2 mL) of fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium. Transfer the resuspended cells to the prepared culture dishes from Step 1, adjusting the volume of medium to achieve the desired cell density.
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine target cell density for optimal growth upon thawing and adjust medium volume accordingly in the culture dishes.
steps.notes: Gentle resuspension is critical to avoid cell damage after centrifugation.

steps.title: Incubate cells for 24 hours
steps.body_md: Place the culture dishes containing the thawed HeLa cells into a humidified cell culture incubator set at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 hours. Allow the cells to recover and re-attach.
steps.notes: The 24-hour incubation allows assessment of sustained viability, not just immediate post-thaw recovery.

steps.title: Harvest cells for viability assessment (post-24h incubation)
steps.body_md: After 24 hours of incubation, carefully remove the medium from the culture dishes. Wash the adherent cells once with sterile PBS. Add an appropriate volume of trypsin-EDTA solution to detach cells (e.g., 1-2 mL per T25 flask or equivalent surface area). Incubate at 37°C for 2-5 minutes, observing cell detachment under a microscope. Once detached, add complete growth medium to inactivate trypsin and collect the cell suspension into a sterile tube.
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine the appropriate volume of trypsin and medium based on your culture dish size/cell confluence.
steps.notes: Visual confirmation of cell detachment is crucial to avoid over-trypsinization (which can harm cells) or insufficient detachment.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Resuspend Harvested Cells
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the collected cell suspension at 200 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature to pellet the cells. Aspirate the supernatant and gently resuspend the cell pellet in a small, defined volume (e.g., 0.5-1 mL) of fresh complete growth medium or PBS, ensuring a single-cell suspension. This uniform suspension is critical for accurate cell counting.
equipment_needed: Sterile tubes (e.g., 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube or 15 mL conical)
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells) or PBS
todo_for_researcher: Specify the exact resuspension volume based on expected cell numbers for optimal counting density.
steps.notes: Accurate resuspension volume and proper mixing are essential for precise cell density determination.

steps.title: Perform cell count and viability assessment using Trypan Blue exclusion
steps.body_md: Take an aliquot of the resuspended cell suspension (e.g., 10-20 µL) and mix it thoroughly with an equal volume of 0.4% Trypan Blue solution. Incubate the mixture for 1-3 minutes at room temperature. Load 10 µL of the stained cell mixture onto a hemocytometer. Using an inverted microscope, count both viable (unstained, clear cytoplasm) and non-viable (stained blue) cells in at least four large corner squares. Calculate the total cell count and percentage of viable cells.
steps.notes: Counting within the 1-3 minute window is critical for accurate Trypan Blue results, as prolonged exposure can lead to uptake by viable cells.

steps.title: Calculate cell viability and density
steps.body_md:
Calculate the total number of viable cells per mL, the total number of non-viable cells per mL, and the overall percentage viability using the following formulas:

% Viability = (Number of viable cells / Total number of cells (viable + non-viable)) * 100
Total Cells/mL = (Average count per square * Dilution factor * 10^4) (where 10^4 is the volume correction for a hemocytometer square)
Viable Cells/mL = Total Cells/mL * (% Viability / 100)

Record all raw counts and calculated values.
steps.notes: Ensure proper dilution factors are applied, including the 1:1 dilution with Trypan Blue.

equipment: Sterile conical tube (e.g., 15 mL or 50 mL)
reagents: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)

success_criteria.how_measured: Percentage of unstained cells via Trypan Blue exclusion on a hemocytometer, 24 hours post-thaw.
success_criteria.threshold: For DMSO control condition, expect >80%. For experimental conditions, observe the measured percentage.

success_criteria.what: Observation of cell attachment and healthy morphology
success_criteria.how_measured: Microscopic observation of adherent HeLa cells in culture dishes after 24 hours. Cells should appear spread out and refractile.
success_criteria.threshold: Qualitative assessment of good attachment and healthy appearance.

steps.title: Thaw and Initiate HeLa Cell Culture
steps.body_md: Retrieve a single cryovial of HeLa cells from liquid nitrogen storage. Rapidly thaw the cryovial by immersing it in a 37°C water bath for approximately 2-3 minutes until only a small ice crystal remains. Transfer the thawed cell suspension to a 15 mL conical tube containing 9 mL of pre-warmed complete growth medium (DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS, 1% Penicillin-Streptomycin).
steps.notes: Ensure quick thawing to minimize exposure to DMSO and maximize viability. Gently mix to avoid cell damage.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Resuspend Cells
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature to pellet the cells. Carefully aspirate the supernatant, leaving approximately 0.5 mL of medium. Resuspend the cell pellet gently by pipetting up and down.
steps.notes: Aspirate supernatant carefully to avoid disturbing the cell pellet. This step removes the cryoprotectant (DMSO).

steps.title: Seed Cells into T-75 Flask
steps.body_md: Add 9.5 mL of fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium to the resuspended cell pellet for a total volume of 10 mL. Transfer the entire cell suspension to a T-75 tissue culture flask. Label the flask with the date, cell type, passage number (P1 for this initial culture), and your initials.
steps.notes: Ensure the medium is pre-warmed to prevent temperature shock to the cells.

steps.title: Incubate Cells
steps.body_md: Place the T-75 flask in a humidified tissue culture incubator maintained at 37°C with 5% CO2. Allow the cells to attach and proliferate. Monitor cell growth daily by visual inspection using an inverted microscope.
steps.notes: Cells should be observed for morphology and confluency.

steps.title: Subculture Cells (Passaging)
steps.body_md: When the T-75 flask reaches 70-80% confluency (typically every 2-3 days), aspirate the old medium. Wash the cell monolayer once with 5 mL of sterile 1x PBS. Aspirate PBS completely. Add 2 mL of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA solution to the flask, ensuring the entire monolayer is covered. Incubate at 37°C for 2-5 minutes until cells begin to detach. Gently tap the flask to aid detachment if necessary.
todo_for_researcher: Determine optimal trypsinization time based on specific HeLa cell line characteristics and manufacturer's recommendations.
steps.notes: Do not over-trypsinize cells, as this can lead to cell damage and reduced viability during subsequent steps.

steps.title: Neutralize Trypsin and Collect Cells
steps.body_md: Once cells are detached, add 8 mL of complete growth medium to the flask to neutralize the trypsin. Gently pipette the cell suspension up and down to break up any cell clumps. Transfer the entire cell suspension to a 15 mL conical tube.
steps.notes: Ensure complete neutralization of trypsin to prevent residual proteolytic activity.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Resuspend for Counting
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature. Aspirate the supernatant completely. Resuspend the cell pellet in 1-2 mL of fresh complete growth medium.
steps.notes: The exact resuspension volume should allow for accurate cell counting.

steps.title: Determine Cell Count and Viability
steps.body_md: Take a 10 µL aliquot of the cell suspension and mix it with 10 µL of 0.4% Trypan Blue solution. Count viable and non-viable cells using a hemocytometer or automated cell counter. Calculate the total cell density (cells/mL) and percentage viability. Ensure cells are within passages 5-15.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm the specific passage number range (P5-P15) is appropriate for preventing phenotypic drift in HeLa cells for reproducibility across experiments.
steps.notes: Cell viability should ideally be >90% for subsequent cryopreservation. Discard cells with low viability (<80%).

steps.title: Prepare Cells for Cryopreservation
steps.body_md: Based on the cell count, centrifuge the remaining cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes. Aspirate the supernatant. Resuspend the cell pellet in fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium to achieve the desired cell density of 1 x 10^6 - 2 x 10^6 cells/mL for cryopreservation.
steps.notes: Accurate cell density is crucial for consistent cryopreservation results across experimental groups.

steps.title: Prepare Trehalose Cryoprotectant Solution (0.5M Trehalose in Basal Medium)
steps.body_md: Weigh out 90.08 g of D-(+)-Trehalose dihydrate. Dissolve the trehalose in approximately 400 mL of the basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS, appropriate for HeLa cells) in a sterile 1 L volumetric flask. Ensure all solids are completely dissolved. Adjust the final volume to 500 mL with basal medium.
reagents_referenced: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS)
steps.notes: The molecular weight of trehalose dihydrate is approximately 360.32 g/mol. For a 0.5M solution in 500 mL, 0.5 mol/L * 0.5 L = 0.25 mol. 0.25 mol * 360.32 g/mol = 90.08 g.

steps.title: Sterilize Trehalose Cryoprotectant Solution
steps.body_md: Filter the 0.5M trehalose solution through a 0.22 µm sterile filter into a sterile bottle. Label the bottle clearly with the solution name, concentration, preparation date, and your initials. Store at 4°C.

steps.title: Prepare Sucrose Cryoprotectant Solution (0.5M Sucrose in Basal Medium)
steps.body_md: Weigh out 85.57 g of D-(+)-Sucrose. Dissolve the sucrose in approximately 400 mL of the basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS) in a sterile 1 L volumetric flask. Ensure all solids are completely dissolved. Adjust the final volume to 500 mL with basal medium.
reagents_referenced: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS)
steps.notes: The molecular weight of sucrose is approximately 342.3 g/mol. For a 0.5M solution in 500 mL, 0.5 mol/L * 0.5 L = 0.25 mol. 0.25 mol * 342.3 g/mol = 85.575 g.

steps.title: Sterilize Sucrose Cryoprotectant Solution
steps.body_md: Filter the 0.5M sucrose solution through a 0.22 µm sterile filter into a sterile bottle. Label the bottle clearly with the solution name, concentration, preparation date, and your initials. Store at 4°C.

steps.title: Prepare DMSO Cryoprotectant Solution (10% v/v DMSO in Basal Medium)
steps.body_md: Aseptically add 50 mL of cell culture grade Dimethyl Sulfoxide (DMSO) to a sterile 500 mL bottle containing approximately 450 mL of basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS). Mix gently by inverting the bottle several times. Do not filter after adding DMSO.
equipment_needed: Sterile serological pipette or equivalent for DMSO dispensing
reagents_referenced: Basal cell culture medium (e.g., DMEM with 10% FBS)
steps.notes: DMSO should be added to the medium, not the reverse, to ensure proper mixing and reduce heat generation. DMSO is typically sterile-filtered by the manufacturer and is not re-filtered after mixing with media to avoid loss or contamination.

steps.title: Store DMSO Cryoprotectant Solution
steps.body_md: Label the bottle clearly with the solution name, concentration, preparation date, and your initials. Store the 10% DMSO solution at 4°C and protect from light.

steps.title: Prepare HeLa cells for cryopreservation
steps.body_md: Harvest HeLa cells and gently resuspend them in the appropriate growth medium (DMEM with 10% FBS, 1% P/S). The total volume should be sufficient to achieve the desired cell concentration before splitting into cryoprotectant specific groups.
todo_for_researcher: Specify exact harvesting method (e.g., trypsinization duration based on cell confluency and cell type characteristics).
steps.notes: Ensure cells are healthy and in log-phase growth prior to harvesting for optimal cryopreservation outcomes.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells and remove supernatant
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature to pellet the cells. Carefully aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the cell pellet.
steps.notes: Gentle aspiration is crucial to prevent cell loss.

steps.title: Adjust cell concentration
steps.body_md: Resuspend the cell pellet in a minimal volume (e.g., 1-2 mL) of the standard growth medium. Count viable cells using a hemocytometer and trypan blue exclusion. Adjust the cell concentration to 2x10^6 cells/mL in standard growth medium.
steps.notes: The target concentration of 2x10^6 cells/mL ensures 1x10^6 cells/vial when 0.5 mL of cell suspension is added to 0.5 mL of cryoprotectant, resulting in 1x10^6 cells/mL in the final cryopreservation mix.

steps.title: Prepare cryoprotectant media
steps.body_md:
Prepare three cryoprotectant solutions: 
1. DMSO control: 20% (v/v) DMSO in standard growth medium. 
2. Sucrose: 1 M sucrose in standard growth medium. 
3. Trehalose: 1 M trehalose in standard growth medium.
All solutions should be filter-sterilized and chilled to 4°C prior to use.
steps.notes: The final concentration after mixing with cells will be 10% DMSO, 0.5M sucrose, and 0.5M trehalose respectively.

steps.title: Aliquot cells into cryovials with cryoprotectants
steps.body_md: For each experimental group (DMSO, Sucrose, Trehalose), rapidly mix 0.5 mL of the 2x10^6 cells/mL cell suspension with 0.5 mL of the corresponding chilled cryoprotectant medium in separate 1.8 mL cryovials. This results in a final volume of 1 mL per cryovial at a concentration of 1x10^6 cells/mL with the desired cryoprotectant concentration (10% DMSO, 0.5M Sucrose, or 0.5M Trehalose). Once mixed, proceed immediately to the freezing steps, minimizing the time the cells are exposed to the cryoprotectant at room temperature to no more than 15 minutes.
steps.notes: Ensure thorough but gentle mixing to evenly distribute cells without causing damage. Label cryovials clearly with the cryoprotectant type, cell line, date, and passage number.

steps.title: Controlled slow freezing (initial cooling)
steps.body_md: Place the cryovials in a polystyrene box (e.g., Mr. Frosty freezing container or similar) or an equivalent controlled-rate freezing device that achieves a cooling rate of approximately -1°C per minute. Immediately place the box at -80°C for 24 hours.
steps.notes: The polystyrene box insulates the cryovials, ensuring a gradual decrease in temperature instead of flash freezing, which is critical for cell viability. Confirm the specific cooling rate of your device.

steps.title: Transfer to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage
steps.body_md: After 24 hours at -80°C, carefully transfer the cryovials from the -80°C freezer directly into a liquid nitrogen storage tank (vapor phase or liquid phase, depending on standard lab practice). Record the location of each cryovial.
steps.notes: Minimize exposure of cryovials to ambient temperature during transfer to prevent warming, which can impact cell viability. Always wear appropriate PPE (cryogloves, lab coat, safety goggles) when handling liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Prepare Water Bath and Medium
steps.body_md: Pre-warm a water bath to the specified thawing temperature. Also, pre-warm a sufficient volume of complete growth medium in a sterile container to the specified temperature for immediate use after thawing.

steps.title: Retrieve Frozen Vial
steps.body_md: Carefully retrieve one cryovial of frozen HeLa cells from liquid nitrogen storage, handling it quickly to minimize temperature fluctuations. Ensure cryo-gloves and face shield are worn for safety.

steps.title: Rapid Thawing
steps.body_md: Immediately immerse the cryovial in the pre-warmed water bath. Gently agitate the vial until only a small ice crystal remains (approx. 1-2 minutes). Avoid prolonged incubation to prevent cryoprotectant toxicity.

steps.title: Disinfect Vial Exterior
steps.body_md: Remove the vial from the water bath and immediately disinfect its exterior with 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Transfer Cells to Conical Tube
steps.body_md: Under sterile conditions in a laminar flow hood, open the disinfected cryovial and carefully transfer its entire contents (typically 1 mL) to a sterile 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Slow Dilution of Cryoprotectant
steps.body_md: Slowly add 9 mL of pre-warmed complete growth medium dropwise to the conical tube containing the thawed cells, over approximately 1-2 minutes. Gently swirl the tube during addition to ensure even mixing and gradual dilution of the cryoprotectant.

steps.title: Centrifugation
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at the specified speed and duration to pellet the cells. Ensure the centrifuge is balanced.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm optimal centrifuge speed for HeLa cells for gentle pelleting post-thaw

steps.title: Remove Supernatant
steps.body_md: Carefully aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the cell pellet. Retention of a small volume (e.g., 50-100 μL) above the pellet is acceptable.
equipment_needed: Vacuum aspirator with sterile Pasteur pipette

steps.title: Resuspend Cells and Transfer to Culture Vessel
steps.body_md: Gently resuspend the cell pellet in an appropriate volume of fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium (typically 1-2 mL, depending on desired density). Transfer the cell suspension to a sterile tissue culture flask or well plate.
todo_for_researcher: Determine specific culture vessel and exact medium volume based on downstream experimental needs.

steps.title: Incubation
steps.body_md: Place the culture vessel in a humidified CO2 incubator at the specified temperature and CO2 concentration to allow cells to recover.
steps.notes: Cell viability will be assessed 24 hours post-thaw according to the experimental plan.

steps.title: Prepare cell culture dishes for post-thaw incubation
steps.body_md: Prepare new cell culture dishes or multiwell plates by adding pre-warmed complete growth medium suitable for HeLa cells (e.g., DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS, 1% Penicillin-Streptomycin). Ensure the culture medium is at 37°C before adding cells.
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine appropriate cell culture dish size/format based on experimental design and cell number.
steps.notes: Ensuring the medium is pre-warmed minimizes thermal shock to the thawed cells.

steps.title: Rapid Thawing of Cryovials
steps.body_md: Retrieve cryovials containing frozen HeLa cells from liquid nitrogen storage. Immediately immerse the cryovial in a 37°C water bath. Agitate gently until only a small ice crystal remains (approximately 1-2 minutes). Avoid prolonged incubation in the water bath once thawed.
steps.notes: Rapid thawing is crucial for cell viability. The small ice crystal indicates thawing is complete but avoids overheating the cells.

steps.title: Transfer thawed cells to pre-warmed medium
steps.body_md: Under sterile conditions in a biological safety cabinet, decontaminate the cryovial exterior with 70% ethanol. Unscrew the cap and immediately transfer the entire contents of the cryovial to a sterile tube containing 5-10 mL of pre-warmed complete growth medium. This dilutes the cryoprotectant.
equipment_needed: Sterile conical tube (e.g., 15 mL or 50 mL)
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine exact dilution volume based on cryovial volume and expected cell concentration.
steps.notes: Gradual dilution of cryoprotectants helps prevent osmotic shock and improves cell recovery.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells to remove cryoprotectant
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 200 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature. This will pellet the cells and allow for removal of the supernatant containing the cryoprotectant.
steps.notes: Pelleting cells gently prevents cellular damage. Room temperature centrifugation is sufficient.

steps.title: Resuspend cells and transfer to culture dishes
steps.body_md: Carefully aspirate the supernatant, ensuring not to disturb the cell pellet. Gently resuspend the cell pellet in a small volume (e.g., 1-2 mL) of fresh, pre-warmed complete growth medium. Transfer the resuspended cells to the prepared culture dishes from Step 1, adjusting the volume of medium to achieve the desired cell density.
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine target cell density for optimal growth upon thawing and adjust medium volume accordingly in the culture dishes.
steps.notes: Gentle resuspension is critical to avoid cell damage after centrifugation.

steps.title: Incubate cells for 24 hours
steps.body_md: Place the culture dishes containing the thawed HeLa cells into a humidified cell culture incubator set at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 hours. Allow the cells to recover and re-attach.
steps.notes: The 24-hour incubation allows assessment of sustained viability, not just immediate post-thaw recovery.

steps.title: Harvest cells for viability assessment (post-24h incubation)
steps.body_md: After 24 hours of incubation, carefully remove the medium from the culture dishes. Wash the adherent cells once with sterile PBS. Add an appropriate volume of trypsin-EDTA solution to detach cells (e.g., 1-2 mL per T25 flask or equivalent surface area). Incubate at 37°C for 2-5 minutes, observing cell detachment under a microscope. Once detached, add complete growth medium to inactivate trypsin and collect the cell suspension into a sterile tube.
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells)
todo_for_researcher: Determine the appropriate volume of trypsin and medium based on your culture dish size/cell confluence.
steps.notes: Visual confirmation of cell detachment is crucial to avoid over-trypsinization (which can harm cells) or insufficient detachment.

steps.title: Centrifuge and Resuspend Harvested Cells
steps.body_md: Centrifuge the collected cell suspension at 200 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature to pellet the cells. Aspirate the supernatant and gently resuspend the cell pellet in a small, defined volume (e.g., 0.5-1 mL) of fresh complete growth medium or PBS, ensuring a single-cell suspension. This uniform suspension is critical for accurate cell counting.
equipment_needed: Sterile tubes (e.g., 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube or 15 mL conical)
reagents_referenced: Complete growth medium (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep for HeLa cells) or PBS
todo_for_researcher: Specify the exact resuspension volume based on expected cell numbers for optimal counting density.
steps.notes: Accurate resuspension volume and proper mixing are essential for precise cell density determination.

steps.title: Perform cell count and viability assessment using Trypan Blue exclusion
steps.body_md: Take an aliquot of the resuspended cell suspension (e.g., 10-20 µL) and mix it thoroughly with an equal volume of 0.4% Trypan Blue solution. Incubate the mixture for 1-3 minutes at room temperature. Load 10 µL of the stained cell mixture onto a hemocytometer. Using an inverted microscope, count both viable (unstained, clear cytoplasm) and non-viable (stained blue) cells in at least four large corner squares. Calculate the total cell count and percentage of viable cells.
steps.notes: Counting within the 1-3 minute window is critical for accurate Trypan Blue results, as prolonged exposure can lead to uptake by viable cells.

steps.title: Calculate cell viability and density
steps.body_md:
Calculate the total number of viable cells per mL, the total number of non-viable cells per mL, and the overall percentage viability using the following formulas:

% Viability = (Number of viable cells / Total number of cells (viable + non-viable)) * 100
Total Cells/mL = (Average count per square * Dilution factor * 10^4) (where 10^4 is the volume correction for a hemocytometer square)
Viable Cells/mL = Total Cells/mL * (% Viability / 100)

Record all raw counts and calculated values.
steps.notes: Ensure proper dilution factors are applied, including the 1:1 dilution with Trypan Blue.

cited_protocols.title: Cryopreservation of C. elegans with DMSO and Trehalose

assumptions: Standard cell culture equipment (incubators, BSCs, centrifuges, microscopes) is available and functional.
Certified aseptic techniques will be maintained throughout all cell culture procedures.
Appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) will be used.
Reagents and media are of cell culture grade and prepared according to manufacturer instructions unless otherwise specified.
An established, healthy stock of HeLa cells is available for initial culture expansion.
